body:
Thanks for your PR, this is something that we did not consider, and thus might not be straightforward to merge, since we need consensus on this feature within the team first. 

Is there a reason you prefer this towards "function" templates? Having a motivation to add this would be ideal

Perhaps this PR is complicated by the fact that it actually introduces 2 changes. I decided to do it that way because both changes were to the same section of code, so if I made separate PRs they would likely conflict with each other.

My motivation for adding support for partial templates is fairly obvious, since it's the reason behind why they were invented in the first place: it allows the template creator to modularize and reduce duplication within a template (for example, if a template displays a name in several places, it can use a "name" partial template to render it in each case).

My main motivation for adding a "render" parameter to function templates was that I was looking for some way for the hits widget to be able to use a different template for each row based on the data in the row (which is a feature others have asked for in the past). The cleanest way I could come up with was to create a function template that was able to choose the template based on the data and then render it--but to do the rendering, it needed all the original context, much of which wasn't available to the function, only to its caller. So I created a render parameter that allowed the render to happen with all the original context.

That's an interesting addition to the current templating system.

> The cleanest way I could come up with was to create a function template that was able to choose the template based on the data and then render it--but to do the rendering, it needed all the original context, much of which wasn't available to the function, only to its caller.

This might mean that we didn't modularize our templates sections enough, which is likely. Could you explain what wasn't possible with a function template exactly here?

Eventually, we want to move away from Hogan and rely on a more powerful renderer though. This change would have been gracefully accepted earlier but we don't think it makes sense to add this feature if we want to get rid of Hogan all together soon.

Thanks a lot for your contribution though – feel free to open an issue first next time to make sure that a change is appropriate to land in the codebase.

> This might mean that we didn't modularize our templates sections enough, which is likely. Could you explain what wasn't possible with a function template exactly here?

What I was trying to do is have two (or more) completely different templates for rendering search hits and choose one based on the value of one of the fields of the item being rendered. I was leaning towards using Hogan templates because they seemed less verbose for fairly complex output, but I guess I'd be open to using a function template instead if that would do what I want. I was also hoping to avoid combining all the templates into a single large function template with an if statement or other logic to choose the relevant part to render.

> Thanks a lot for your contribution though – feel free to open an issue first next time to make sure that a change is appropriate to land in the codebase.

No problem. I thought since I had figured out how to do it, it made sense to submit it as a PR. So you're not interested in either part of the PR? The Mustache partial implementation could be pretty useful until you actually do move away from Hogan, and the render parameter could be adapted to provide something like partials for function templates even after you do move away from Hogan (by changing it so that you pass a template name instead of the full content of the template to the render function).

Please ignore my earlier comments about function templates--for some reason when I first read about them, I failed to realize they were full JS functions using string interpolation, and my thinking hadn't fully adjusted when I wrote that comment. Being a JS function, it can of course call any other function template that is also a JS function.

I've simplified this PR by removing the render parameter support. Now it only adds partial support to Hogan templates which, as you'll see, is a very simple change, a matter of making the template list available to Hogan. It's still subject to infinite recursion, which I have a possible plan for dealing with if you would like me to attempt it. If you like, I could also resubmit this PR on a new branch without the complication of the other stuff in the history.

On the other hand, if you're simply not interested in expanding Hogan support for partials at this time, that's fine too--simply reject this PR, and thanks for your consideration.

Thanks for the efforts @glassfordm, we really appreciate it.

As explained in https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/4133#issuecomment-534290126, since what you're trying to achieve can be done with JavaScript functions and that we don't plan to invest more in Hogan, we'll close this PR.
